expectations.text: behavioral_check: Recommends writing the correlation matrix to an `AnalysisNwbfile` and storing only an `*_object_id` (varchar(40)) plus `analysis_file_name` reference in the DJ row — does NOT store the matrix as a `longblob` / `blob` column
expectations.evidence: Computed table definition stores `-> AnalysisNwbfile`, `ccg_object_id: varchar(40)`, etc. Explicitly: 'too big for a DataJoint blob column. The DJ row stores only file name + object ids.'

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Names the SpyglassMixin-first MRO requirement (`class X(SpyglassMixin, dj.Computed)`) as a non-negotiable — the validator pins this with a regression fixture
expectations.evidence: 'SpyglassMixin first in MRO, with SpyglassMixinPart for the part table (Non-Negotiable #1).' All schema classes use the SpyglassMixin-first form.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Cites the non-negotiable that tables reference exactly ONE AnalysisNwbfile table (Spyglass validates on declaration); the row carries the file name + object IDs, not the heavy array itself
expectations.evidence: 'Output goes to AnalysisNwbfile (Non-Negotiable #4)... The DJ row stores only file name + object ids.' One `-> AnalysisNwbfile` reference per table.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Uses the `AnalysisNwbfile().build(nwb_file_name)` context manager for the create -> add -> register lifecycle, OR an equivalent atomic shape — does not interleave separate `create()` / `add_nwb_object()` / `add()` calls
expectations.evidence: make() uses `with AnalysisNwbfile().build(nwb_file_name) as builder: ...` and inside calls `builder.add_nwb_object(...)`. No separate create/add interleaving.
